data.name: txtHelp.Text
data.value: ########################### [ BIN X ] Quantity of specific Bin Grade (X : Bin Sequence) ex) $_BIN_1 --------------------------------------- [ Total ] Total quantity of source Lot ex) $_TOTAL --------------------------------------- [ Oper In ] Current Operation in quantity of source Lot ex) $_OPERIN --------------------------------------- [ BOper In ] Passed Operation in quantity of source Lot ex) $_BOPER_3030_$ --------------------------------------- [ CASE WHEN ~ THEN ~ END ] select value by conditions ex) CASE WHEN $_BIN_1 > 1000 THEN 20 WHEN $_BIN_2 >= 2 THEN 14 WHEN $_BIN_1 < 300 THEN 2 END ########################### [ ABS(n) ] Returns the absolute value of N. --------------------------------------- [ EXP(n) ] Returns e raised to the Nth power, where e = 2.71828183... --------------------------------------- [ MOD(n,m) ] Returns remainder of M divided by N. Returns M if N is 0. --------------------------------------- [ POWER(n,m) ] Returns M raised to the Nth power. The base M and the exponent N can by any numbers, but if M is negative, N must be an integer. --------------------------------------- [ ROUND(n,m) ] Returns N rounded to M places right of the decimal point. M defaults to 0. M can be negative to round off digits left of the decimal point. M must be an integer. --------------------------------------- [ SQRT(n) ] Returns square root of N; if N < 0 then NULL --------------------------------------- [ SIGN(n) ] if N < 0 then -1; if N = 0 then 0; if N > 0 then 1 --------------------------------------- [ LN(n) ] Returns the natural logarithm of N where N > 0 --------------------------------------- [ LOG(n,m) ] Returns the logarithm, base M, of N. The base M can be any positive number other than 0 or 1 and N can be any positive number. --------------------------------------- [ TRUNC(n,m) ] Returns N truncated to M decimal places; M defaults to 0. M can be negative to truncate (make zero) M digits left of the decimal point. ########################### [ SIN(n) ] Returns the sine of N (an angle expressed in radians). --------------------------------------- [ COS(n) ] Returns the cosine of N (an angle expressed in radians). --------------------------------------- [ TAN(n) ] Returns the tangent of N (an angle expressed in radians). --------------------------------------- [ SINH(n) ] Returns the hyperbolic sine of N. --------------------------------------- [ COSH(n) ] Returns the hyperbolic cosine of N. --------------------------------------- [ TANH(n) ] Returns the hyperbolic tangent of N. --------------------------------------- [ ASIN(n) ] Returns the arc sine of N. Inputs are in the range of -1 to 1, and outputs are in the range of -pi/2 to pi/2 and are expressed in radians. --------------------------------------- [ ACOS(n) ] Returns the arc cosine of N. Inputs are in the range of -1 to 1, and outputs are in the range of 0 to pi and are expressed in radians. --------------------------------------- [ ATAN(n) ] Returns the arc tangent of N. Inputs are in an unbounded range, and outputs are in the range of -pi/2 to pi/2 and are expressed in radians. --------------------------------------- [ ATAN2(n) ] Returns the arc tangent of N and M. Inputs are in an unbounded range, and outputs are in the range of -pi to pi, depending on the signs of x and y, and are expressed in radians. Atan2(x,y) is the same as Atan2(x/y). ###########################
